title: Code Structure Deep Dive
subtitle: Write Better, Smarter Arduino Programs!
overview: Students will explore the structure of Arduino programs, understand the purpose of functions, variables, constants, and compare delay() with millis() to create efficient and organized code.

images._note: Add screenshots of Arduino IDE, coding examples, and classroom activities.

keyTopics: Best Practices for Writing Clean Code

youtubeVideos.title: Arduino Program Structure Explained
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn about setup() and loop() functions.

youtubeVideos.title: Arduino Variables and Constants
youtubeVideos.desc: Understanding variables and constants in Arduino.

youtubeVideos.title: delay() vs millis() Tutorial
youtubeVideos.desc: Why millis() is often better than delay().

activity.title: Convert Blink Code into Modular Code
steps: Create a constant for LED pin
Create a custom function called blinkLED()
Move LED ON/OFF code into the function
Replace delay() with millis() timing logic

quiz.q: Which function runs only once when Arduino starts?
quiz.explanation: setup() executes only once when the Arduino powers up or resets.
loop() continuously runs after setup() finishes.

options: A storage location for data that can change
quiz.explanation: Variables store values that may change during program execution.

quiz.q: Which keyword is commonly used to create a constant?
quiz.explanation: The const keyword makes a value fixed and unchangeable.
Functions improve readability and reduce duplicate code.

quiz.q: What is a drawback of delay()?
options: It blocks other code from running
quiz.explanation: delay() pauses the entire program during the waiting period.
millis() returns the number of milliseconds since the board started.

quiz.q: Why is millis() preferred for advanced projects?
options: It allows multiple tasks to run without blocking

quiz.q: Which is an example of a constant?
quiz.explanation: A constant value should not change during program execution.

quiz.q: What is the benefit of meaningful variable names?
quiz.explanation: Clear variable names make programs easier to understand and maintain.

quiz.q: Which data type stores whole numbers?
quiz.explanation: int is commonly used to store integer values.
Modular programming breaks large programs into smaller reusable functions.

quiz.q: Which timing method is better for controlling multiple LEDs independently?
quiz.explanation: millis() allows independent timing without blocking other tasks.

quiz.q: What is the main goal of good code structure?
options: Make code organized, reusable, and maintainable
quiz.explanation: Well-structured code is easier to understand, debug, and expand.
